pi_items.source: Windows path format validation; matches env.json user account 'Docker' and standard Windows Documents folder structure
Task description explicitly states 'Install an array of at least 4 solar panels'; consistent with typical 2x2 grid solar installations

Official NCH DreamPlan documentation (help.nchsoftware.com, DreamPlan versions page, and add-ons KB article) shows no mention of 'Solar Panel' as a built-in object. Exterior tab categories documented as: Plants, Lighting, Paths, Pools, Fencing, Furniture, Accessories, Misc. DreamPlan version history (up to version 9.28, Sept 2024) contains no mention of solar panel features. Blog post about importing 3D models does not reference solar panels.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real software (NCH DreamPlan by NCH Software) and a real sample project (Contemporary House). The expected output path C:\Users\Docker\Documents\solar_project.dplan is a valid Windows path format consistent with the Docker user account defined in the environment. The minimum panel requirement of 4 is explicitly stated in the task description and represents a reasonable solar installation size (2x2 grid pattern). However, the claimed 'Solar Panel' object does not appear to exist in DreamPlan's documented built-in object library as of the latest version (9.28, September 2024). Official NCH documentation lists Exterior > Accessories and Roof categories but does not enumerate 'Solar Panel' as an available object. This suggests the task may require custom 3D model imports, which are supported by DreamPlan but not documented in the metadata.
